risks.risk_title: Phishing and Social Engineering via RBI Brand Impersonation
risks.description: Attackers impersonate RBI through emails, SMS, or phone calls to extract sensitive investor information such as bank account details or passwords.
risks.regulatory_reference: RBI Master Directions — Caution notice on impersonation

risks.domain: Access Control — Third-Party and Agent Management
risks.risk_title: Unauthorized Broker Activity and Fraudulent Brokerage Claims
risks.description: Unregistered or deactivated brokers submit applications or claims using invalid or stolen broker codes, resulting in fraudulent payments.
risks.regulatory_reference: RBI Master Directions — Section A: Appointment / Delisting of Brokers

risks.domain: Access Control — Third-Party and Agent Management
risks.risk_title: Misuse of RBI Brand by Sub-Agents
risks.description: Sub-agents appointed by Receiving Offices falsely claim RBI endorsement in marketing materials, misleading investors and exposing Receiving Offices to regulatory and reputational liability.
mitigating_controls: Contractual brand-use prohibition in sub-agent agreements
Periodic audit of agent marketing materials
risks.regulatory_reference: RBI Master Directions — Section A.2: Appointment of sub-agents

risks.description: Brokerage claims are not settled within the mandated 30-day window, leading to agent disputes, regulatory findings, and reputational damage.
risks.regulatory_reference: RBI Master Directions — Section B.3: Brokerage claims

risks.description: Failure to submit accurate monthly remittance reports or Appendix IV statements results in unreconciled advance payments and adverse audit findings.
mitigating_controls: Appendix IV statement submission for 10% balance
risks.regulatory_reference: RBI Master Directions — Section B.3d: Reimbursement centralized at CAS Nagpur

risks.risk_title: Missing or Invalid Nomination Records for Bond Holders
risks.description: Bond proceeds are disbursed to the wrong party or disputed in court because nominations were never registered, were registered after maturity, or were not updated following a bond transfer.
mitigating_controls: Mandatory nomination registration with written acknowledgement
risks.regulatory_reference: RBI Master Directions — Section C: Nomination Facility

risks.description: Nomination records are altered without the bond holder's written consent, enabling a fraudulent beneficiary to claim bond proceeds.
mitigating_controls: Written change requests with date-stamped registration
Audit log of all nomination events
risks.regulatory_reference: RBI Master Directions — Section C.4: Variation/cancellation of nomination

risks.risk_title: Invalid NRI Nomination Under Prohibited Bond Scheme
risks.description: A Non-Resident Indian is registered as a nominee under the 7.75% Savings (Taxable) Bonds 2018 scheme where NRI nominations are explicitly prohibited, creating a FEMA compliance violation.
mitigating_controls: Scheme-specific NRI eligibility checklist at point of registration
Staff training on active scheme restrictions
Rejection log for non-compliant nomination requests
risks.regulatory_reference: RBI Master Directions — Section C.8: NRI nomination restrictions

risks.risk_title: Operations Based on Outdated Master Directions
risks.description: Staff or Receiving Offices apply superseded rules because internal policy documents were not updated simultaneously with the publication of revised RBI Master Directions.
mitigating_controls: Designated policy owner monitors RBI publications
Version-controlled policy documents with revision dates
Mandatory distribution acknowledgement from all Receiving Offices
risks.regulatory_reference: RBI Master Directions — Foreword: simultaneous update obligation
